questions.stem:
—I ______ my pen. Have you seen it?
—When ______ you ______ it?
questions.explanation: 第一空强调过去动作对现在的影响（笔现在还在丢着的状态），用现在完成时have lost；第二空询问过去发生的具体时间，用一般过去时did...lose。解题技巧：现在完成时侧重'影响/结果'，一般过去时侧重'具体时间'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my brother.
questions.explanation: 先行词the boy指人，且在定语从句中作主语，关系代词用who。which指物；whose表所属；whom指人但只能作宾语。口诀：人物主who宾whom，物用which所有whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new bridge ______ by the end of last month.
questions.explanation: by the end of last month（到上个月底为止）是过去完成时的标志词，且bridge与build是被动关系，故用过去完成时的被动语态had been built。A是现在完成时；C是一般过去时；D是一般现在时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Do you think ______ we will have a picnic tomorrow?
—I'm not sure. It ______ rain.
questions.explanation: 第一空根据答语I'm not sure可知表示'是否'，可用if或whether引导宾语从句；第二空表示不确定的推测（可能性较小的猜测），用might。must表示肯定推测（一定），can表示能力或理论可能性，均不符合语境。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you ______ the prefix 'un' from 'unhappy', you get the word 'happy'.
questions.explanation: remove意为'移除、去掉'。句意：如果你去掉unhappy中的前缀un，就得到了happy。add添加；put放置；build建立，均不符合句意。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you are ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: 根据后半句'你应该更努力以取得更好的成绩'，可推断前半句表示'对当前成绩不满意'。dissatisfied意为'不满意的'。excited兴奋的；surprised惊讶的；tired疲倦的，均不符合逻辑。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ______ a solution to the environmental problem.
questions.explanation: come up with意为'想出、提出（主意、计划等）'，符合句意'提出解决方案'。look forward to期待；get on with与……相处/继续做；run out of用完，均不符合搭配。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: attract吸引。句意：这是一个吸引了许多学生和老师的激动人心的活动。invite邀请（主语通常是人）；visit参观；join加入（组织/活动），但此处表示'吸引'更为恰当。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held a Science Fair. It was an exciting event that (1)______ many students and teachers. As a member of the organizing committee, I was responsible for setting up the exhibition hall.

On the morning of the fair, I arrived early. The hall was large, but we needed to (2)______ the tables and chairs properly. My classmates and I worked together to move the heavy equipment. Although we felt tired, we knew it was (3)______ the effort.

When the visitors started coming in, I noticed a boy standing in front of our project. He looked confused. I walked over and asked if he (4)______ help. He told me he couldn't understand how our solar-powered car worked. I explained the process patiently, using simple words to make it clear.

After listening to my explanation, his eyes lit up. "This is amazing!" he said. "I want to build something like this too." His words made me feel proud. I realized that (5)______ knowledge with others is a wonderful experience.

The Science Fair was a great success. Not only did we show our inventions, but we also learned how to communicate scientific ideas effectively.

questions.explanation: arrange安排、布置。根据上下文'搭建展厅'，需要'布置'桌椅。buy购买；design设计；repair修理，均不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:

After listening to my explanation, his eyes lit up. "This is amazing!" he said. "I want to build something like this too." His words made me feel proud. I realized that (5)______ knowledge with others is a wonderful experience.

The Science Fair was a great success. Not only did we show our inventions, but we also learned how to communicate scientific ideas effectively.

questions.explanation: be worth the effort意为'值得这份努力'，是固定搭配。句意：尽管我们感到累，但知道这是值得的。necessary必要的；important重要的；possible可能的，虽然语法通顺，但worth最符合英语表达习惯。

questions.group_passage:

questions.explanation: 根据上文'他看起来很困惑'，我走过去问他是否'需要'帮助。need需要；offer主动提供；refuse拒绝；accept接受，均不符合语境逻辑。

questions.group_passage:

questions.explanation: share sth. with sb.意为'与某人分享某物'。根据上文我给男孩讲解科学知识，可知此处指'与他人分享知识'。receive收到；test测试；check检查，均不符合文意。

questions.stem: What is the new technology mentioned in the passage?
options: A. A machine that cleans oceans.
B. An enzyme that breaks down plastic quickly.
C. A method to heat plastic safely.
D. A way to make plastic softer.
questions.explanation: 细节题。根据第二段第一句'The team has created a special enzyme that can break down plastic bottles in just a few hours.'可知，新技术是一种能快速分解塑料的酶。A选项清洁海洋的机器文中未提及；C、D只是过程中的步骤，不是核心技术。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is one of the biggest environmental problems we face today. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste end up in our oceans, harming marine life (海洋生物) and ecosystems. However, a new technology developed by a team of scientists might offer a solution.

The team has created a special enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few hours. Traditional recycling methods often take months to degrade (降解) plastic. This new enzyme works much faster and can be used repeatedly. "It's like having a super-powered recycling machine," says Dr. Zhang, the lead researcher.

The process is surprisingly simple. First, the plastic is heated to make it soft. Then, the enzyme is added. Within hours, the plastic turns into its original chemical components (成分). These components can then be used to make new plastic products. This means we could create a circular economy where plastic is continuously reused instead of being thrown away.

However, some experts warn that this technology is not a magic fix. "We still need to reduce our plastic use," says environmentalist Liu Wei. "No matter how good the recycling technology is, producing less waste is always better."

Despite these concerns, the discovery gives hope for a cleaner future. If this technology can be widely used, it could significantly reduce the amount of plastic waste in our environment. The team is now working with companies to make the process more affordable for large-scale use.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Liu Wei's words in Paragraph 4?
options: A. He thinks the technology is useless.
B. He believes recycling is more important than reducing waste.
C. He thinks people should still use less plastic.
D. He wants to stop the research immediately.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第四段Liu Wei的话'We still need to reduce our plastic use... producing less waste is always better'可知，他认为即使有了回收技术，人们仍然应该减少塑料使用。A与原文相反；B与原文'减少比回收更好'矛盾；D文中未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is one of the biggest environmental problems we face today. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste end up in our oceans, harming marine life (海洋生物) and ecosystems. However, a new technology developed by a team of scientists might offer a solution.

The team has created a special enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few hours. Traditional recycling methods often take months to degrade (降解) plastic. This new enzyme works much faster and can be used repeatedly. "It's like having a super-powered recycling machine," says Dr. Zhang, the lead researcher.

The process is surprisingly simple. First, the plastic is heated to make it soft. Then, the enzyme is added. Within hours, the plastic turns into its original chemical components (成分). These components can then be used to make new plastic products. This means we could create a circular economy where plastic is continuously reused instead of being thrown away.

However, some experts warn that this technology is not a magic fix. "We still need to reduce our plastic use," says environmentalist Liu Wei. "No matter how good the recycling technology is, producing less waste is always better."

Despite these concerns, the discovery gives hope for a cleaner future. If this technology can be widely used, it could significantly reduce the amount of plastic waste in our environment. The team is now working with companies to make the process more affordable for large-scale use.

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "components" in Paragraph 3 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据第三段'These components can then be used to make new plastic products'可知，这些components可以用来制造新产品，推断其意为'成分、组成部分'。component=part/element。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is one of the biggest environmental problems we face today. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste end up in our oceans, harming marine life (海洋生物) and ecosystems. However, a new technology developed by a team of scientists might offer a solution.

The team has created a special enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few hours. Traditional recycling methods often take months to degrade (降解) plastic. This new enzyme works much faster and can be used repeatedly. "It's like having a super-powered recycling machine," says Dr. Zhang, the lead researcher.

The process is surprisingly simple. First, the plastic is heated to make it soft. Then, the enzyme is added. Within hours, the plastic turns into its original chemical components (成分). These components can then be used to make new plastic products. This means we could create a circular economy where plastic is continuously reused instead of being thrown away.

However, some experts warn that this technology is not a magic fix. "We still need to reduce our plastic use," says environmentalist Liu Wei. "No matter how good the recycling technology is, producing less waste is always better."

Despite these concerns, the discovery gives hope for a cleaner future. If this technology can be widely used, it could significantly reduce the amount of plastic waste in our environment. The team is now working with companies to make the process more affordable for large-scale use.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Plastic Invention
B. How to Reduce Plastic Use
C. New Hope for Plastic Recycling
D. The Dangers of Marine Life
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章主要介绍了一种能快速分解塑料的新酶技术，为塑料回收带来新希望。A塑料发明史，偏离主题；B如何减少使用，只是文末提及；D海洋生物危险，只是开头背景。C选项准确概括了文章核心——新技术给塑料回收带来希望。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Plastic pollution is one of the biggest environmental problems we face today. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste end up in our oceans, harming marine life (海洋生物) and ecosystems. However, a new technology developed by a team of scientists might offer a solution.

The team has created a special enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few hours. Traditional recycling methods often take months to degrade (降解) plastic. This new enzyme works much faster and can be used repeatedly. "It's like having a super-powered recycling machine," says Dr. Zhang, the lead researcher.

The process is surprisingly simple. First, the plastic is heated to make it soft. Then, the enzyme is added. Within hours, the plastic turns into its original chemical components (成分). These components can then be used to make new plastic products. This means we could create a circular economy where plastic is continuously reused instead of being thrown away.

However, some experts warn that this technology is not a magic fix. "We still need to reduce our plastic use," says environmentalist Liu Wei. "No matter how good the recycling technology is, producing less waste is always better."

Despite these concerns, the discovery gives hope for a cleaner future. If this technology can be widely used, it could significantly reduce the amount of plastic waste in our environment. The team is now working with companies to make the process more affordable for large-scale use.

options: A. However, drinking too much coffee is bad for you.
B. Then, remember to stay hydrated.
C. Even 30 minutes of activity can make a difference.
D. This means you should avoid too much junk food like burgers and chips.
E. Going to bed early helps you wake up refreshed and ready to learn.
questions.explanation: 前文提到睡眠，E选项'早睡帮助你神清气爽地醒来并准备好学习'承接上文。后文'Without enough rest'也与E选项形成呼应。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many teenagers want to stay healthy, but they don't know where to start. Here are some simple tips that can help you develop healthy habits.

First, try to get enough sleep. Most teenagers need about 8-10 hours of sleep each night. (16)______ Without enough rest, you will feel tired and find it hard to concentrate in class.

Second, eat a balanced diet. (17)______ Instead, choose fruits, vegetables and whole grains. These foods give you energy and help your body grow strong.

Third, exercise regularly. You don't need to run a marathon. (18)______ Walking to school, playing basketball, or even dancing in your room counts. The key is to move your body every day.

(19)______ Drinking enough water helps your skin look good and keeps your brain working well. Try to drink at least 8 glasses of water a day.

By following these simple steps, you can improve your health and feel better every day.

options: A. However, drinking too much coffee is bad for you.
B. Then, remember to stay hydrated.
C. Even 30 minutes of activity can make a difference.
D. This means you should avoid too much junk food like burgers and chips.
E. Going to bed early helps you wake up refreshed and ready to learn.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是'均衡饮食'，D选项'这意味着你应该避免太多垃圾食品如汉堡和薯条'符合主题。后文'Instead, choose fruits...'与D选项形成对比转折关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

Third, exercise regularly. You don't need to run a marathon. (18)______ Walking to school, playing basketball, or even dancing in your room counts. The key is to move your body every day.

(19)______ Drinking enough water helps your skin look good and keeps your brain working well. Try to drink at least 8 glasses of water a day.

By following these simple steps, you can improve your health and feel better every day.

options: A. However, drinking too much coffee is bad for you.
B. Then, remember to stay hydrated.
C. Even 30 minutes of activity can make a difference.
D. This means you should avoid too much junk food like burgers and chips.
E. Going to bed early helps you wake up refreshed and ready to learn.
questions.explanation: 本段主题是'锻炼'，前文说'不需要跑马拉松'，C选项'即使30分钟的活动也能带来改变'承接上文，说明少量运动也有用。后文列举的步行、打篮球等活动也印证了这一点。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many teenagers want to stay healthy, but they don't know where to start. Here are some simple tips that can help you develop healthy habits.

First, try to get enough sleep. Most teenagers need about 8-10 hours of sleep each night. (16)______ Without enough rest, you will feel tired and find it hard to concentrate in class.

Second, eat a balanced diet. (17)______ Instead, choose fruits, vegetables and whole grains. These foods give you energy and help your body grow strong.

Third, exercise regularly. You don't need to run a marathon. (18)______ Walking to school, playing basketball, or even dancing in your room counts. The key is to move your body every day.

options: A. However, drinking too much coffee is bad for you.
B. Then, remember to stay hydrated.
C. Even 30 minutes of activity can make a difference.
D. This means you should avoid too much junk food like burgers and chips.
E. Going to bed early helps you wake up refreshed and ready to learn.
questions.explanation: 后文提到'喝足够的水帮助你的皮肤看起来好并让大脑运转良好'，可知本段主题是'补水'。B选项'Then, remember to stay hydrated（保持水分充足）'作为段落主题句最合适。A选项虽然提到喝，但是关于咖啡，与后文水无关。
